proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not (overall, unconditional on HER2 status).
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not (overall).
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months.
stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
has_brain_mets=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0.

analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib +1.10 mo PFS vs no palbociclib (mean 5.42 vs 4.32, n=17428 vs 32572, Welch t-test).
Trastuzumab overall: PFS difference -0.060 mo (n=10039 vs 39961). Slight negative direction overall, weakly significant.
Pembrolizumab overall: PFS difference -0.074 mo (n=7585 vs 42415).
Each unit increase in ECOG PS (0/1/2) is associated with -1.18 months PFS.
Stage IV patients have PFS 1.54 months shorter than non-stage-IV.
Patients with brain metastases have PFS 1.05 months shorter than those without (p=1.5e-178).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_trastuzumab and her2_positive on pfs_months: trastuzumab benefit is larger in HER2-positive than HER2-negative patients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca1_mutation on pfs_months: olaparib benefit is larger in BRCA1-mutated than BRCA1 wild-type patients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation on pfs_months: olaparib benefit is larger in BRCA2-mutated patients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_palbociclib and er_positive on pfs_months: palbociclib benefit is larger in ER-positive than ER-negative patients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high on pfs_months: pembrolizumab benefit is larger in MSI-high patients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_tamoxifen and er_positive on pfs_months: tamoxifen benefit is larger in ER-positive patients.

analyses.result_summary: trastuzumab*her2_positive interaction is null. Stratified: trastuzumab effect among HER2+ = -0.079 mo (p=0.15) and among HER2- = -0.051 mo (p=0.10). No HER2-conditional benefit detected.
olaparib*brca1_mutation interaction coefficient +0.45 mo, borderline.
olaparib*brca2_mutation interaction coefficient +0.34 mo, not significant.
palbociclib*er_positive interaction is highly significant: +1.59 months additional PFS in ER+ vs ER- patients on palbociclib.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ pembro + msi + pembro*msi; stratified analysis
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.22 mo, p=0.47. Stratified: pembrolizumab among MSI-high diff +0.14 mo (n=86 vs 412, p=0.64), among MSI-low diff -0.08 (p=0.015). No detectable MSI-high benefit.
tamoxifen*er_positive interaction coefficient is small and non-significant (-0.058 mo).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
Within er_positive=0 patients, treatment_palbociclib does NOT increase pfs_months (null effect outside ER+).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months in this cohort (testing whether older patients have longer or shorter PFS).
Higher fatigue_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months (symptom burden as prognostic).

analyses.code: ttest pfs by palbo within ER+
analyses.result_summary: ER+ subgroup: palbociclib +1.58 mo PFS (n=12194 vs 22692, p~0).

analyses.code: ttest pfs by palbo within ER-
analyses.result_summary: ER- subgroup: palbociclib effect -0.017 mo, not significant.
Each +1 g/dL of albumin associated with +0.50 months PFS (p=9e-112).
Each +1% weight loss in 6 mo associated with -0.075 months PFS.
Each +1% ki67 associated with -0.019 months PFS.
Each +1 U/L LDH associated with -0.0005 months PFS (small but precise; over 200 U/L range that is ~-0.1 mo).

Each +1 year of age associated with +0.176 months PFS in this cohort. (Direction is positive; older patients have longer PFS in these data, possibly because younger patients have more aggressive subtypes here.)
Fatigue grade has near-zero slope (+0.0005 mo per grade), not associated with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs by race_ethnicity category.
Mean pfs_months differs by insurance_type category (medicare vs medicaid vs private vs uninsured).
rural_residence=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than rural_residence=0.
Higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months.
sex_female=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than sex_female=0.
Higher bmi is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: F=0.85, p=0.49. Means: white 4.68, asian 4.74, black 4.72, hispanic 4.71, other 4.76. No detectable disparity in PFS by race/ethnicity.
F=0.31, p=0.82. Means: medicaid 4.71, private 4.69, medicare 4.69, uninsured 4.73. No detectable insurance disparity.
Rural vs urban PFS difference +0.027 mo, not significant.
Slope -0.003 mo per year of education, not significant.
Female vs male PFS difference -0.013 mo, not significant.
Slope +0.004 mo per BMI unit, borderline non-significant.
Slope -0.0003 mo per pack-year, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: pik3ca_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than pik3ca_mutation=0.
her2_low=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than her2_low=0.
tp53_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than tp53_mutation=0.
diabetes_mellitus=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than diabetes_mellitus=0.
heart_failure=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than heart_failure=0.
prior_chemotherapy=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than prior_chemotherapy=0.
liver_mets=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than liver_mets=0.
prior_malignancy=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than prior_malignancy=0.

analyses.result_summary: PIK3CA-mut patients have PFS 0.56 months SHORTER than wild-type (p~6e-144). Strong main effect.
HER2-low patients have PFS 0.11 months LONGER than non-HER2-low (p~1e-6).
TP53 mutation: diff +0.007 mo, no detectable effect.
Diabetes diff +0.022 mo, not significant.
Heart failure diff +0.016 mo, not significant.
Prior chemotherapy diff +0.002 mo, not significant.
Liver mets diff +0.018 mo, not significant despite biological expectation.
Prior malignancy diff -0.047 mo, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within er_positive=1 patients, the palbociclib PFS benefit is reduced or abolished when pik3ca_mutation=1 (i.e., negative 3-way interaction palbo*er_positive*pik3ca_mutation on pfs_months).
Within er_positive=1 patients, the palbociclib PFS benefit is reduced when her2_positive=1 (palbo*er*her2 negative interaction).
Within er_positive=1 patients, the palbociclib PFS benefit is enhanced when her2_low=1 (positive palbo*er*her2_low interaction).
Within er_positive=1 patients, the palbociclib PFS benefit is similar regardless of stage_iv status.
The negative main effect of pik3ca_mutation on pfs_months is concentrated in ER+ patients (interaction pik3ca*er_positive).

analyses.code: Stratified palbo effect within ER+ by PIK3CA; OLS palbo*er*pik3ca three-way
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+, palbo effect is +2.38 mo when PIK3CA-wt vs +0.04 mo when PIK3CA-mut (interaction p=2.8e-151 in ER+ stratum). Three-way palbo*er*pik3ca coef -2.43 mo, p=1.6e-128, confirming PIK3CA mutation abolishes the palbociclib benefit within ER+ patients.

analyses.code: Stratified palbo effect within ER+ by HER2
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+, palbo effect +1.92 mo when HER2- vs +0.02 mo when HER2+ (interaction p~3e-151).

analyses.code: Stratified palbo effect within ER+ by HER2-low
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+, palbo effect +1.94 mo when HER2-low vs +1.36 mo when not HER2-low. Modest positive interaction (p=1e-23).

analyses.code: Stratified palbo effect within ER+ by stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+, palbo effect +1.54 mo (stage IV) vs +1.60 mo (non-stage IV). Interaction p=0.30; benefit is similar across stage strata.

analyses.code: Stratified PIK3CA effect by ER status
analyses.result_summary: PIK3CA effect on PFS: ER+ subset diff -0.80 mo (p~2e-192), ER- subset diff ~0.00 mo (p=0.99). PIK3CA is harmful only within ER+ patients - consistent with the loss of palbociclib responsiveness mechanism.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months in HER2-positive patients (any ER status) when restricted to that subgroup.
Sacituzumab govitecan is associated with longer pfs_months among triple-negative (ER-/PR-/HER2-) patients.
Pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months among triple-negative patients.
Olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months among patients carrying brca1_mutation OR brca2_mutation (combined BRCA1/2 carrier subgroup).
Tamoxifen is associated with longer pfs_months among ER-positive AND non-postmenopausal patients (premenopausal ER+).

analyses.code: Stratified trastuzumab effect by HER2 x ER
analyses.result_summary: Among HER2+/ER+: trastuzumab effect -0.087 mo (p=0.18); HER2+/ER-: -0.063 (p=0.55); HER2-/ER+: -0.031 (p=0.43); HER2-/ER-: -0.090 (p=0.06). No evidence of trastuzumab benefit in any HER2/ER stratum - signal absent in this dataset despite biological expectation.

analyses.code: Stratified sacituzumab effect by triple_neg and her2_low
analyses.result_summary: Sacituzumab among triple-negative: diff -0.085 mo (n=1084 vs 9412, p=0.22); HER2-low: -0.031 mo (p=0.64). No detectable benefit in either expected subgroup.

analyses.code: Stratified pembrolizumab effect by triple_neg and msi_high
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab among triple-negative diff +0.072 mo (p=0.22); among msi_high diff +0.14 mo (n=86 vs 412, p=0.64). Direction matches biology in TN subset but not statistically supported; in non-TN subset diff is -0.114 mo (p=0.002), consistent with no overall benefit and possibly mild harm in unselected use.

analyses.code: Stratified olaparib effect by BRCA1/2 carrier status; OLS interaction olap*brca_any
analyses.result_summary: Combined BRCA1/2 carriers: olaparib +0.346 mo (n=239 vs 2260, p=0.041); non-carriers: -0.056 mo (p=0.14). Interaction olap*brca_any coef +0.40 mo, p=0.021. Modest but statistically supported PARP-inhibitor benefit confined to BRCA carriers.

analyses.code: Stratified tamoxifen effect by ER x postmenopausal
analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen by stratum: ER+/post +0.010 (p=0.81); ER+/pre +0.044 (p=0.38); ER-/post +0.063 (p=0.19); ER-/pre +0.109 (p=0.07). All directions positive but none significant; no clear ER+/premenopausal advantage emerged.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 24 SNPs (snp_rs*) is associated with pfs_months at conventional significance after Bonferroni correction.
snp_rs1801131 is associated with shorter pfs_months (lowest unadjusted p among SNPs).
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher calcium_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months.
prior_surgery=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than prior_surgery=0.

analyses.result_summary: Across 24 SNP main effects, smallest unadjusted p is 0.026 (snp_rs1801131). After Bonferroni correction (0.05/24=0.002), no SNP reaches significance. No germline genotype tested explains pfs_months variation.
snp_rs1801131 minor allele carrier (=1) has PFS -0.054 mo lower than non-carrier; nominal p=0.026, not significant after multiple-testing correction.
Slope -0.0007 mo per U/L; statistically significant (p=0.003) with very small magnitude.
Slope -0.064 mo per mg/dL calcium, significant (p=0.004).
Prior surgery vs no prior surgery: PFS diff +0.066 mo (p=0.005). Small but real positive association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model that includes ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, age_years, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, ki67_pct, ldh_u_l, her2_low, pik3ca_mutation, er_positive, treatment_palbociclib, palbo*er, palbo*er*(1-pik3ca), and olaparib*BRCA, the palbo*er*(1-pik3ca) interaction term is the dominant treatment-related predictor of pfs_months.

After adjusting for tumor and host factors, the marginal main effects of treatment_palbociclib, treatment_olaparib, and the bare palbo*er interaction are NOT significant once the three-way palbo*er*PIK3CA-wild-type term is included.
her2_low retains an independent positive association with pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ ecog + stage + brain + age + albumin + weight_loss + ki67 + ldh + her2_low + pik3ca + er + palbo + palbo*er + palbo*er*(1-pik3ca) + olap*BRCA
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable model R^2=0.844. The palbo*er*(1-pik3ca) coefficient is +2.39 months (p~0). The bare treatment_palbociclib (+0.003), palbo*er (-0.000), olap*BRCA (-0.013) coefficients are negligible and non-significant. The benefit of palbociclib is concentrated entirely in the ER+/PIK3CA-wild-type stratum.

Bare palbo coefficient is +0.003 (p=0.88); palbo*er is -0.0003 (p=0.99); olap*BRCA is -0.013 (p=0.84). Confirmed: with the three-way interaction in the model, no other treatment-related main or two-way coefficient reaches significance.

analyses.code: Coefficient of her2_low in multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted her2_low coefficient is +0.131 mo (p~6e-47). Independent positive association persists after adjustment for tumor/host factors and treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the ER+/PIK3CA-wild-type stratum, the palbociclib PFS benefit is further restricted to her2_positive=0 patients (i.e., palbo benefits ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt but not ER+/HER2+/PIK3CA-wt).
There is a positive interaction between ecog_ps and stage_iv on pfs_months: stage IV further worsens PFS more in lower-ECOG patients (or attenuates differently across ECOG strata).
There is a negative interaction between age_years and stage_iv on pfs_months: the protective association of older age is reduced in stage IV patients.
All four cells of the 2x2 ER x PIK3CA grid show palbociclib effects of distinct magnitudes, with benefit concentrated only in ER+/PIK3CA-wt.

analyses.code: Stratified palbo effect within ER+/PIK3CA-wt by HER2
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+/PIK3CA-wt: palbociclib effect +2.91 mo when HER2- (n=6571 vs 12110, p~0); -0.017 mo when HER2+ (n=1463 vs 2644, p=0.81). Palbociclib benefit is sharply restricted to ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt patients.
ecog*stage_iv interaction coef +0.067, p=0.040. Small positive interaction: stage IV penalty is slightly less in higher-ECOG patients (perhaps because both already truncate PFS).
age*stage_iv interaction coef -0.0112 mo per year-of-age x stage-IV (p=3.1e-12). The positive association of age with PFS is significantly attenuated in stage IV patients.

analyses.code: Stratified palbo effect across ER x PIK3CA cells
analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib effects: ER+/PIK3CA-wt +2.376 (p~0, n=8034 vs 14754); ER+/PIK3CA-mut +0.035 (p=0.39, n=4160 vs 7938); ER-/PIK3CA-wt -0.048 (p=0.28, n=3371 vs 6476); ER-/PIK3CA-mut +0.039 (p=0.52, n=1863 vs 3404). Strong concentration of benefit in ER+/PIK3CA-wt cell only.
